lessons.text: Receive sacred wisdom handed down through teachers.
lessons.rationale: The yoga passed from teacher to student in unbroken succession, revived when lost.

lessons.text: Restore what is right whenever it declines.
lessons.rationale: The divine descends age after age to protect the good and renew righteousness.

lessons.text: Know the divine truly and end rebirth.
lessons.rationale: Seeing the divine birth and actions in their true light frees one from returning.

lessons.text: Burn attachment, fear, and anger in knowledge.
lessons.rationale: Those purified by the fire of knowledge and freed of these three attain the divine.

lessons.text: Others meet you the way you approach them.
lessons.rationale: In whatever way people approach the divine, so are they answered.

lessons.text: Take decisive action when you seek worldly success.
lessons.rationale: Those who long for results act and sacrifice, for success comes quickly through action.

lessons.text: Work knowing action never taints the Self.
lessons.rationale: The Self is the non-doer, untainted and free of desire for the fruits of action.

lessons.text: Study action and inaction to win freedom.
lessons.rationale: Even the wise are confused about action and inaction; understanding them liberates.

lessons.text: See inaction in action and action in inaction.
lessons.rationale: The one who perceives this is wise, a yogi amid all his doing.

lessons.text: Let knowledge burn desire from your work.
lessons.rationale: The sage's undertakings are free of selfish desire, consumed by the fire of knowledge.

lessons.text: Drop hope and craving while you act.
lessons.rationale: Abandoning attachment to fruits and controlling the mind, one acts yet incurs no sin.

lessons.text: Stay even-minded in success and failure.
lessons.rationale: Content with what comes unsought and free of opposites, one acts unbound.

lessons.text: Work for sacrifice and your karma dissolves.
lessons.rationale: For the detached mind fixed in knowledge, action done as sacrifice melts away entirely.

lessons.text: See the divine present in every act.
lessons.rationale: Brahman is the offering, the fire, and the doer for one who sees it in all action.

lessons.text: Offer your senses and breath as sacrifice.
lessons.rationale: Diverse yogis sacrifice senses, objects, breath, and study in the fire of restraint.

lessons.text: Give of yourself, for without sacrifice nothing is gained.
lessons.rationale: This world is not for the non-sacrificer; those who share the remnants reach the eternal.

lessons.text: Know every sacrifice is born of action.
lessons.rationale: Recognizing all sacrifices as born of action, one is liberated.

lessons.text: Prize wisdom above ritual; all action ends in knowledge.
lessons.rationale: Knowledge-sacrifice is superior to material sacrifice; every action culminates in knowledge.

lessons.text: Approach the wise with humility and service.
lessons.rationale: The realized teach knowledge to those who prostrate, question, and serve.

lessons.text: See every being within your own Self.
lessons.rationale: With this knowledge you perceive all beings in yourself and in the divine.

lessons.text: Cross even the worst sins by the raft of knowledge.
lessons.rationale: However sinful, one crosses over all sin by knowledge.

lessons.text: Let knowledge reduce your karma to ash.
lessons.rationale: Like fire turning fuel to ash, the fire of knowledge consumes all actions.

lessons.text: Nothing purifies you like true knowledge.
lessons.rationale: There is no purifier in this world equal to knowledge.

lessons.text: Pair faith with restraint to gain lasting peace.
lessons.rationale: The faithful, devoted, and sense-controlled attain knowledge and then supreme peace.

lessons.text: Release doubt, which destroys your happiness.
lessons.rationale: The ignorant and doubting perish, finding no happiness in this world or the next.

lessons.text: Cut doubt with the sword of knowledge and rise.
lessons.rationale: Slay the doubt born of ignorance with self-knowledge, take refuge in yoga, and act.

skipped.reason: Narrative: Arjuna's question about the timeline of the teaching.
Narrative/theological: the divine recalls its many past births.
Theological statement about the unborn divine taking form through Maya; no actionable directive.
